lynx-web.description: Lynx for Web in Chromium. Real worker BG + postMessage IPC. Primary product scale.

bare-dom.description: Plain browser DOM (Preact / Vue / Vapor). Same host, no Lynx. Use to bound framework-only cost.

node-jitless.description: Node V8 --jitless PAPI stubs. Strategy microbenchmark — NOT comparable to browser FCP.

architectures.label: Vue Vapor dense (Named Tree, no IFR)
Vue Vapor+IFR engine-et paint (STUB on web)

meta.note: plain DOM, single-threaded (no Lynx); same click-driven storm protocol as cross.mjs

verdicts.claim: Vapor wins update ops 5.8–9.8× on BG thread vs VDOM (instrumented).
verdicts.detail: Instrumented BG select still ~9.8×. Black-box selectStorm@10k reproduces ~8.3×. One-shot select@10k is near parity (1.19×) — frame-floor masks BG wins.

verdicts.claim: Vapor creation is roughly parity with VDOM (slightly slower e2e).
verdicts.detail: create@10k vapor/vdom=1.00×. React still leads create@10k (react/vdom=0.96×).

verdicts.claim: Default IFR wins median −19% FCP on real threads (content scene).
verdicts.detail: Holds for ~1k ×1 content probe; fails as a scale/CPU-invariant claim. −19% is a mid-size ×1 content-scene result, not a universal constant. vdom-ifr@1k ×1: -21%. @10k ×1: 33%. @30k ×1: 29%. @1k ×4: 12%. IFR+ET@30k ×1: 36%.

verdicts.claim: Element Templates are the render-cost inflection; IFR without ET is dangerous at scale.
verdicts.detail: IFR-sans-ET slower than off at 10k, 20k, 30k. IFR+ET best at 1k, 3k, 5k, 10k, 20k. Caveat: on tiny real examples (hello-world), IFR alone can beat IFR+ET on web FCP — ET tax before win.

verdicts.claim: React leads creation; Vue/Vapor lead sustained updates (storms).
verdicts.detail: React leads create@10k. Vapor selectStorm@10k ≪ React (119 vs 2632 ms). vdom-ifr/vdom selectStorm@10k=1.01× (plain IFR ≈ off: OK). vdom-ifr-et/vdom selectStorm@10k=0.98×, create@10k=0.97×.

verdicts.claim: Single-process FCP is flat across IFR configs — proves dual-thread necessity.
verdicts.detail: Keep as methodology proof that single-process harnesses cannot show IFR benefit. Do not cite flat FCP as product evidence. Real-thread (lynx-web) + native are the only win scales.

verdicts.claim: The three benchmarks are on comparable scales / can be read as one story.
verdicts.detail: Four distinct scales coexist: (1) instrumented BG/e2e ms, (2) black-box click→composed-DOM, (3) lynx-web FCP/settled, (4) node --jitless warm render. Same ladder labels (1k–30k) do NOT make (1)–(4) interchangeable. Unified schema tags environment+workload; docs must not ratio across environments.
